mechanism_narrative: A high and persistent unemployment rate above 8% signals significant economic slack and depressed consumer demand, leading to lower corporate earnings expectations and negative forward returns for the S&P 500.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE is 9.6, well above historical averages
failure_condition: The S&P 500 exhibits a positive median 60-day forward return during periods when the unemployment rate is above 8% for at least three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is pinned near the zero-lower-bound, accommodative monetary policy reduces the discount rate for future earnings and encourages risk-taking, driving positive equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS is 0.19, indicating a zero-interest-rate policy (ZIRP) is active
failure_condition: The information coefficient (IC) of the FEDFUNDS level signal on 20-day forward returns is negative or statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) during ZIRP periods.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent slowdown in both retail sales growth and housing starts indicates a broad-based contraction in household spending and investment, which negatively impacts corporate revenues and equity valuations over the medium term.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year growth in both RSAFS and HOUST is negative.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST value of 598.0 is historically low
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where both retail sales and housing starts growth are negative year-over-year.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive momentum in industrial production reflects a resilient or recovering supply side of the economy, leading to improved earnings outlooks and positive short-term equity performance.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO value of 93.2093 is below the 2017 baseline of 100
failure_condition: A signal based on the 3-month change in INDPRO fails to achieve a positive Sharpe ratio over a 5-year rolling backtest window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market, signaled by a sharp increase in nonfarm payrolls job losses, precedes a wider economic downturn and causes a negative revision to equity risk premia.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE is at a cycle high of 9.6
failure_condition: The hit rate (percentage of correct directional predictions) of the signal for negative 20-day returns is less than 55% in a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation, measured by the year-over-year change in CPI, increases input costs for firms and signals potential future monetary tightening by the central bank, both of which are negative for equity prices.
failure_condition: The correlation between the 12-month change in CPIAUCSL and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession environment, a divergence where real GDP growth turns positive while employment growth remains negative signals a 'jobless recovery'. This boosts corporate profit margins through high productivity, leading to positive equity returns.
candidate_trigger: Quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth > 0% AND quarter-over-quarter PAYEMS growth < 0%
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE is at a very high level of 9.6
failure_condition: The median 60-day S&P 500 return is negative during quarters that satisfy the trigger condition.

mechanism_narrative: An unexpected positive monthly change in nonfarm payrolls has an outsized positive impact on short-term market sentiment when the unemployment rate is already high, as it signals a potential turning point in a weak labor market.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 7.0% AND monthly change in PAYEMS > 50k
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE is 9.6, satisfying the regime condition
failure_condition: The average 5-day forward return following a trigger event is not statistically different from the average 5-day return on non-trigger days (t-test p > 0.05).
